header.title: Unix Timestamp Converter — Epoch to Date & Time Online
header.description: Free online Unix timestamp converter to convert between epoch timestamps and human-readable dates. View current Unix time, convert across time zones, and format dates in ISO, UTC, and local time. Essential for developers working with APIs and databases.
header.keywords: unix timestamp converter, epoch converter, convert unix timestamp, epoch to date, timestamp to date, unix time converter, epoch time online, date to timestamp

enterTimestamp: Enter a timestamp or pick a notable one

howToUse.title: How to Use the Unix Timestamp Converter
howToUse.step1Desc: Select Timestamp to Date or Date to Timestamp depending on what you want to convert.
howToUse.step2Desc: Type a Unix timestamp or pick a date using the date picker fields.
howToUse.step3Desc: Choose between Seconds (standard Unix) or Milliseconds (JavaScript-style).
howToUse.step4Desc: View the converted output in local time, UTC, ISO 8601, and other common formats.

items.question: What is a Unix timestamp?
items.answer: A Unix timestamp is the number of seconds (or milliseconds) that have elapsed since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC.

items.question: Is there a difference between seconds and milliseconds?
items.answer: Yes. JavaScript's Date.now() returns milliseconds (13 digits), while most APIs use seconds (10 digits).

items.question: Can I see the current Unix timestamp?
items.answer: Yes. The tool displays the current Unix time in real-time at the top of the page.

items.question: Does this support time zones?
items.answer: Yes. You can view results in both your local time zone and UTC, along with ISO 8601 and RFC 2822 formats.
